markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Feed code symbol neighborhoods into bounded context bundles with citations and token accounting. This MT belongs to CodeIndexingAndNavigation. Index code structure and expose precise backend navigation for agents.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Code navigation must use Handshake-managed parsers/adapters and store spans/citations in PostgreSQL.
Tree-sitter is the first parser family; LSP/SCIP-style precision is a Handshake-owned import/projection path, not an outside service requirement.
scope.expected_behavior: CodeContextBundleBridge leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: CodeContextBundleBridge is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 working tree: code-index implementation hardened after EventLedger JSONB idempotency false-conflict; real PostgreSQL tests green (knowledge_code_index_tests 22, knowledge_code_index:: lib 82, knowledge_code_nav_api_tests 2) plus MT-109 exact stress 25/25; adversarial re-review PASS 2026-06-14.

concerns: OUTPUT bounded, WORK unbounded: the token budget caps how many neighbors are EMITTED, but build_code_context_bundle first fetches ALL references edges of the focus (context_bridge.rs:119 list_knowledge_edges_for_entity, no cap) and does a get_knowledge_entity + first_ast_span round-trip for EVERY neighbor (context_bridge.rs:134-141) BEFORE the budget truncation runs (context_bridge.rs:190). A hot symbol called by 10k sites does 10k+ DB queries and builds 10k CitedNeighbor structs, then throws most away. The pathological symbol graph the attack posits DOES blow the WORK budget (DB + memory), even though the model's context window is protected. The budget is an output guardrail, not a work guardrail.

Focus always emitted in full regardless of budget: used = focus_tokens.min(budget) (context_bridge.rs:179) clamps the accounting, but the focus's display_name/key/doc are written into allowed_context unconditionally (context_bridge.rs:231-244). If focus_doc is large the bundle exceeds token_budget. Mitigated in practice because doc display_name is truncated to ~120 chars at ingestion (truncate_snippet), but the budget is not actually enforced on the focus.

Token estimate is chars/4 (context_bridge.rs:40) and counts only display_name + entity_key per neighbor, NOT the actual snippet/source text (the bundle carries no source text, only keys+line ranges). So 'estimated_tokens' undercounts what a consumer that later fetches the spans will spend - the budget protects the bundle JSON size, not the eventual hydrated context. Honest as a guardrail but the name overstates it.
Recursion: NONE - only direct calls/called_by neighbors are included; no transitive expansion. The unbounded-recursion probe is refuted.
evasion_probes: Confirmed single-hop neighborhood only (context_bridge.rs:121-160): edges touching focus, relation calls/called_by, no recursive descent into neighbors. Cannot recurse unboundedly.

Confirmed deterministic packing: neighbors sorted, dedup by (relation,key), packed smallest-first within budget, truncated flag recorded in token_accounting (context_bridge.rs:247-254). A re-run yields the same bundle.
Confirmed staleness is attached (context_bridge.rs:105 file_staleness -> staleness_label in allowed_context) so a stale neighborhood is labeled.
Confirmed the bundle is the kernel's existing durable ContextBundle (ContextBundle::new) and the module writes no rows itself - matches the 'projection only' constraint.
evidence_checked: delegate tests: token estimate determinism + cited-neighbor json (logic-only, no large-fanout test)

hardening_closed.note: Closed for READY_FOR_VALIDATION. Prior HARDEN_RECOMMENDED concerns remain recorded in adversarial_code_review and are non-blocking follow-up.

evidence: EventLedger idempotency false-conflict root cause confirmed as PostgreSQL JSONB payload numeric normalization while canonical payload_hash matched.
append_kernel_event idempotency now compares canonical metadata plus payload_hash instead of raw JSONB Value equality; detectors accept enriched conflict diagnostics.
Adversarial re-review PASS: no blocking findings for MT-101/102/107/109/110/111/112 readiness.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-110 (CodeContextBundleBridge) requires projecting a code symbol's neighborhood into a bounded, cited, staleness-labeled context bundle wrapped in the kernel's durable ContextBundle, sourced from the PostgreSQL-backed knowledge index. The implementation in context_bridge.rs does exactly this with real PostgreSQL proof, deterministic token-budget packing, per-neighbor span citations, fail-closed staleness, and projection-only semantics; it aligns with Master Spec 2.3.13.11.
findings: context_bridge.rs:85-271 build_code_context_bundle resolves the focus symbol, gathers single-hop calls/called_by neighbors from References edges through KnowledgeStore (real PostgresDatabase), and emits a bounded allowed_context JSON with per-neighbor span_id + line_start/line_end citations (context_bridge.rs:154-171, 217-226) and a token_accounting block (truncated flag, neighbors_total/included) (context_bridge.rs:259-266). Matches spec 2.3.13.11 bounded cited context requirement.

Staleness is fail-closed: file_staleness (context_bridge.rs:314-345) defaults to StalenessVerdict::MarkedStale when the file entity, primary_source_id, or code-file row is missing, and the label is stamped into allowed_context (context_bridge.rs:242). Matches spec 2.3.13.11.1 machine-readable staleness verdict, fail-closed.

Projection-only: the module writes no durable rows; it wraps the result in the kernel's real durable ContextBundle::new (kernel/context_bundle.rs:18-43, content-hash-addressed CTX- id). Matches the packet/MT constraint that context bundles are projections, not authority.

Real-resource proof: tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs:108-165 stands up genuine managed PostgreSQL (POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL/ManagedPostgres), runs the full migration chain into an isolated schema, with NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback (SKIP only when PG binaries are truly absent). MT-110 tests (knowledge_code_index_tests.rs:1509-1661) index real Rust source through the engine, call build_code_context_bundle over eng.db(), and assert: cited called_by neighbor present, token usage <= budget, path-mismatch rejection, and over-budget focus-doc surfaced as truncated=true. Satisfies sub-rule 2.

Sub-rule 1: grep of context_bridge.rs and its test block found no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/deferred/pending-MT escapes on the live path. Sub-rule 3: contract has claimed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER, completed_by=null, status READY_FOR_VALIDATION (no self-cert to COMPLETED).

Non-blocking (recorded adversarial_code_review HARDEN_RECOMMENDED, superseded by adversarial_review_v2 PASS): list_knowledge_edges_for_entity fetches all focus edges uncapped and does an N+1 get_knowledge_entity per neighbor before budget truncation (context_bridge.rs:131-172) — output is bounded but WORK is not, so a 10k-caller hot symbol does heavy DB work. This is an honest performance follow-up, not a correctness or spec violation, and does not affect MT-110 acceptance.
Aside (non-blocking, expected during governance wind-down): the contract cites v02.192 spec anchors; the same section numbers exist and resolve cleanly in current v02.194. Not a failing reason.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
